title: Readiness probe gates on router-load, not just HTTP 200 (issue #165)
intent: Issue #165: the readiness harness lied on a degraded boot. /health returns HTTP 200 with zero checks, and /api/health returns HTTP 200 even when routers fail to load (degradation is only a body field). So an HTTP-200-only readiness check passed on a boot where routers did not load, and api_tests probed /health first (a do-nothing 200) which short-circuited any router signal. Add an optional --require JSON body assertion to the shared utility and gate the router-dependent jobs on /api/health with routers.loaded>0. CI/governance plumbing only; no product behavior change, no endpoint semantics changed, no startup validation weakened.

diff_articulation.what_changed: (1) wait_for_api_ready.py: probe() now also returns the response body; new optional --require 'dotted.key OP value' (ops ==,!=,>=,<=,>,<) is parsed (parse_requirement) and evaluated against the JSON body (check_requirement) so a 200 only counts as ready if the body assertion also holds; the failure witness now prints the failed-gate reason alongside the 200 (e.g. 'HTTP 200 - require routers.loaded>0: got 0'). Backward compatible: with no --require, behavior is unchanged (200 = ready). (2) test_wait_for_api_ready.py: +6 tests (parse variants, _compare numeric/string, check_requirement cases, degraded-boot gated NOT ready, healthy boot ready, failure-output names the gate) — 15 total, stdlib-only. (3) run_api_smoke_posts.sh + api_tests.yml + core_ci.yml (both Boot API jobs) + api_health_check.yml: probe /api/health with --require 'routers.loaded>0' (quoted so the shell does not treat > as redirection) and drop the /health-first ordering that let the zero-check endpoint short-circuit router gating.
diff_articulation.why_not_redundant: An HTTP-200-only readiness check is structurally blind to a degraded boot: neither /health nor /api/health returns a non-200 when routers fail. This makes 'ready' mean 'routers actually loaded', not merely 'ASGI app serving'. routers.loaded>0 is the false-negative-proof floor (a normal multi-router CI boot has loaded>0, proven by #164's router-dependent jobs passing; a total router-load collapse has loaded==0) and catches the #165 catastrophic case; the utility's --require is general so tightening to routers.failed==0 is a one-line change once a clean-boot failed count is confirmed on CI. Endpoint semantics are untouched (Option C, per the dev order's guardrail against changing health endpoints).

commands_run: python scripts/ci/test_wait_for_api_ready.py -> 15 passed (9 prior + 6 new require tests incl. degraded-boot gated NOT ready, healthy boot ready)
yaml.safe_load of api_tests/core_ci/api_health_check workflows -> parse OK
bash -n scripts/smoke/run_api_smoke_posts.sh -> OK; --require value single-quoted so > is not shell redirection

note: real-app degraded-vs-healthy boot is an Ubuntu-CI witness (local Windows app import exceeds 2 min); the gate logic is witnessed locally against a stdlib http.server returning degraded/healthy bodies

files.intent: Add optional --require JSON body assertion so a 200 on a degraded boot (routers.loaded==0) is not treated as ready; backward compatible without it
files.behavior_change: none - CI tooling only; default behavior unchanged when --require absent

files.intent: Cover --require parsing/comparison/evaluation and the degraded-boot gating
Both Boot API jobs gate on /api/health routers.loaded>0
Readiness gate on /api/health routers.loaded>0 before the POST smoke
